learn.title: Bifacial Solar Panels Explained
learn.intro: Bifacial solar panels capture sunlight on both sides, boosting energy yield without extra land. As costs fall, they are key to cheaper clean power. IRENA highlights them as vital for scaling solar globally and reaching net-zero targets efficiently.
learn.c1_text: Bifacial panels use glass on both sides to absorb direct sun and reflected light from ground. Like a solar panel wearing sunglasses on the back, it catches bounce-light. They can yield 5–20% more energy than one-sided panels in same space.
learn.c2_text: Mounting bifacial panels on white gravel or tracking systems increases reflection. Technical detail: albedo (reflectivity) of surface drives rear gain. With 0.3 albedo, rear output may add 10% versus monofacial, improving project returns.
learn.c3_text: India's Bhadla solar park uses bifacial modules to maximise output in Rajasthan's high-albedo desert. With 10 GW of such parks planned, bifacial tech supports India's 280 GW RE target by 2030 using less land per MW.
learn.stat1: Bifacial panels add 5–20% energy vs monofacial
learn.stat3: India solar capacity crossed 90 GW in 2024

learn.title: Demand Response Balances Grid
learn.intro: Demand response lets large users cut or shift power use during peaks, avoiding blackouts and fossil peakers. The US DOE shows it as a low-cost grid tool. With more renewables, flexible demand is essential to match variable supply.
learn.c1_text: Big factories or malls reduce load when grid is stressed, like dimming lights during a party to avoid blowing fuses. They get paid to do so. US DOE notes 30 GW of DR potential can replace expensive emergency generators.
learn.c2_text: Utilities send price or reliability signals via smart meters. Technical: aggregator software curtails HVAC or EVs within seconds. This balances 60 Hz frequency, preventing collapse when solar drops at sunset without new wires.
learn.c3_text: India's POSOCO and DISCOMs run emergency DR in peaks; Maharashtra shifted 1 GW industrial load in 2023. Smart meters under RDSS (250M by 2025) enable automated response, aiding 500 GW RE integration by 2030.
learn.stat1: US DOE: DR can offset 30 GW peak
learn.stat3: Maharashtra shifted 1 GW in DR event 2023
learn.stat4: India aims 500 GW RE capacity by 2030

learn.title: Battery Management Systems
learn.intro: A BMS protects batteries from overheating, overcharge and ageing, making storage safe and long-lasting. US DOE stresses BMS for EV and grid batteries. Good BMS unlocks renewable storage, cutting diesel and stabilising solar-wind power.
learn.c1_text: BMS is the battery's bodyguard, like a thermostat stopping a kettle from boiling dry. It measures voltage and temperature. DOE says it prevents fires; a 60 kWh EV pack needs cell balancing within 0.02 V to avoid early failure.
learn.c2_text: BMS controls state-of-charge between 20–80% to extend cycles. Technical: coulomb counting estimates remaining life. By limiting depth-of-discharge, a grid battery may deliver 6000 vs 3000 cycles, halving storage cost per kWh.
learn.c3_text: India's PLI scheme backs 50 GWh battery manufacturing with local BMS. Ola Electric uses in-house BMS for EVs; BESS at Rajasthan solar parks (500 MWh) rely on BMS for frequency regulation under ISGS norms.
learn.stat1: DOE: BMS prevents thermal runaway fires

learn.title: Carbon Markets Incentives
learn.intro: Carbon markets put a price on emissions, rewarding clean energy via tradable credits. IEA reports show private finance scales with such signals. They channel capital to solar and wind, making pollution costly and green power profitable.
learn.c1_text: A credit equals 1 tonne CO2 avoided, like a coupon for not polluting. Polluters buy them; RE projects sell. IEA notes voluntary market hit $2B in 2022, funding 500 MW clean capacity via offset revenues globally.
learn.c2_text: EU ETS charges ~€80/tonne, pushing utilities to renewables. Technical: cap-and-trade lowers cap yearly. IEA says clear pricing cut EU power emissions 30% since 2005, proving markets drive private clean investment.
learn.c3_text: India's CCTS launched 2023 for compliance trading. Gujarat solar earns credits sold to coal plants. With 100 Mt CO2 covered, it may raise $1B yearly, funding 2 GW RE and meeting Panchamrit net-zero by 2070.
learn.stat2: EU ETS price ~€80 per tonne CO2

learn.title: Geothermal Energy Basics
learn.intro: Geothermal taps Earth's heat for constant power and heating, unlike intermittent sun or wind. US DOE calls it a firm renewable. It can decarbonise industry and grids, providing 24/7 clean baseload with tiny land use.
learn.c1_text: Geothermal uses underground heat like a giant natural water heater. Wells bring 150°C steam up. DOE says US plants give 3.7 GW steady power; one well can heat 1000 homes, running day and night without fuel.
learn.c2_text: Steam spins a turbine; binary plants use low-temp fluid. Technical: ORMAT recovers 10% of heat to electricity at 120°C. Unlike solar, capacity factor is 90%, giving reliable fill for variable renewable grids.
learn.c3_text: India's Ladakh and Tattapani (Chhattisgarh) have 10 GW geothermal potential. ONGC drilled Tattapani well (180°C) for pilot. Under NREDC, 1 MW plant planned by 2025 to heat greenhouses and cut diesel in cold regions.

learn.title: Direct Air Carbon Capture
learn.intro: DAC removes CO2 straight from air using filters and heat, best powered by renewables. IEA 2022 report says DAC is crucial for net-zero. Though energy-intensive, it offsets hard-to-abate sectors and stores carbon permanently.
learn.c1_text: DAC fans pull air through sorbent that grabs CO2 like a sponge absorbs water. IEA says 1 plant captures 4000 t/year. Renewable heat at 100°C releases pure CO2 for storage, needing 2 MWh per tonne.
learn.c2_text: Using geothermal or solar heat cuts net emissions. Technical: Climeworks uses 200°C waste heat. IEA notes 18 DAC units worldwide in 2022; scaling to 60 Mt by 2030 requires 300 TWh clean energy annually.
learn.c3_text: India's CO2 value chain taskforce eyes DAC with surplus solar. Gujarat's Kutch (high solar) proposed pilot capturing 10 kt/year by 2027. Under MOPNG, stored CO2 may boost EOR or make green methanol for ships.
learn.stat2: DAC needs ~2 MWh per tonne CO2
